item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. Got a few links to propose here. It was brought to my attention by weryl, that the D-Class used in the log here, D-19060, is a recurring character in a few of weryl's articles, namely SCP-3435. Simple matter to in-line link from here to there and allow those to follow the path of this particular D-class. Additionally, the disembodied voice present at the end of this skip, with its positive comment, and the contagious nature of SCP-3132, are reminiscent of SCP-1937, which can be linked to as well. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Subject(s): D-19060 and D-28888

To this:

Subject(s): D-19060 and D-28888

Changing this:

<Closing Statement> Dr. Juarez and the two D-class were injured and suffered partial hearing loss, but survived. While most of the recording devices were destroyed by the blast, analysis from the two that survived managed to make out a sound emanating from the epicenter. As of the most recent analysis, the sound was similar to a voice saying the words "Oh, that's nice". More importantly, the shockwave was heard by a large number of site personnel, and has turned out to carry SCP-3132's infectious properties as well. - Site-208 Director Villasana

To this:

<Closing Statement> Dr. Juarez and the two D-class were injured and suffered partial hearing loss, but survived. While most of the recording devices were destroyed by the blast, analysis from the two that survived managed to make out a sound emanating from the epicenter. As of the most recent analysis, the sound was similar to a voice saying the words "Oh, that's nice".1 More importantly, the shockwave was heard by a large number of site personnel, and has turned out to carry SCP-3132's infectious properties as well. - Site-208 Director Villasana

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Based upon the content of the comment, as well as SCP-3132's contagious nature, an investigation into the possibility of SCP-3132 and SCP-1937 being variations of the same phenomenon are ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this be keter? If there wasn't anyone effected then it'd be safe but since there's a bunch of people affected and incurable it could go critical and the amnestic bregade would need to convince everyone spines just work that way.

item.title: Update
item.content:encoded: I've substantially redone the ending. Also a couple infinitesimal touch-ups elsewhere in the article. Maybe this works a little better, I dunno, we'll see. If not I'll come back to it some other time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have a point and you're not the first to make it. I'm seriously considering chopping those off entirely, and have begun work on a new ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This telled too much and showed too little. The last note was just far too blatant for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

This is a great example of "Show, don't tell." Yeah, you spelled it out for us with in that final note, but you got us there way beforehand. I especially liked the foreshadowing with the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No idea how that slipped by. Fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hits a really nice low-key weird vibe that I love.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the simple-anomaly-has-implications style of this; it reminds me of good series 1-2 articles of yesteryear.

Just fyi I noticed this and didn't change it because I couldn't tell the intent:

Never forget that even the most benign, mundane anomalies can pose a problem if proper containment being maintained at all times

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't always use D-class, but when I do I try to humanize them a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice. I loved the little touch of one of the D-Class having a nickname.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhh shit 😩😂 what a 👌🏻💯 QUALITY 😤 skip it’s so 💪🏻💪🏻 Good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mad man. I cannot believe you did this. A hearty +1.

item.title: "OH, THAT'S NICE"
item.content:encoded:
God it feels so good to actually post something again. They say wish-fulfillment skips are frowned upon but we'll see.

Anyway, major thanks to:

TomatointheMirror - This is all your fault and I'm glad it worked out in the end
DrBleep - for crit
FuelTank does not match any existing user name - for pointing out what was unclear since I get really bad at that once i look at the draft long enough
The Great Hippo - for a lot of good ideas, some of which I ended up using
RecursiveRecursion, taylor_itkin, ghostchibi - last readers
My brother - for his constant back cracking that led to me deciding to actually take up tomato on his challenge

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Vertebra_-_thoracicae_(from_below).jpg CC0 1.0
